items.text: For instance, several states have passed legislation commonly referred to as Extended Producer Responsibility.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new regulatory development (Extended Producer Responsibility laws) not previously mentioned, representing a new compliance risk.

items.text: These laws are intended to shift the cost of recycling from consumers to producers while also mandating increased supply, which could lead to lower commodity prices.
items.rationale: Elaborates on the impact of the newly introduced EPR laws, detailing potential cost shifts and commodity price effects.

items.text: At the U.S. state level, California has taken action to require greenhouse gas emission disclosure, as well as information on climate-related financial risks, some of which are subject to legal challenge.
items.rationale: Identifies a specific new regulatory requirement (California climate disclosure law) that creates a distinct compliance and litigation risk.

items.text: Similar bills have been introduced in other U.S. states; to-date, none have passed into law.
items.rationale: Merely notes that similar bills exist elsewhere but have not passed, adding little substantive new risk beyond the already-described California law.

items.text: In April 2025, the Canadian federal government effectively eliminated the fuel charge but the output-based pricing system remains.
items.rationale: Provides an update on an existing regulatory framework (Canadian carbon pricing) reflecting a change in its scope rather than a wholly new risk.

items.text: Our operations are increasingly dependent on technology, including AI and machine learning tools that we deploy or that are embedded in systems provided by third parties.
items.rationale: Introduces a new operational risk related to reliance on AI and machine learning technologies, not previously disclosed.

items.text: AI models and tools can produce inaccurate, biased, or inconsistent outputs and may rely on third-party content or training data for which we do not have sufficient rights.
items.rationale: Further details the risks associated with the newly introduced AI dependency, such as inaccurate outputs and data rights issues.
